The backbone of the orcish horde. Individually weak but dangerous in numbers.
Grunts are slow and predictable. Use chokepoints to maximize ballista efficiency against groups.
Cunning archers who strike from a distance, softening defenses before the main assault.
Bow Orcs stop to fire, making them vulnerable to ballista shots. Prioritize them before they whittle down your defenders.
Massive brutes with an insatiable hunger for destruction. They target walls with single-minded fury.
Trolls ignore defenders and beeline for walls. Station pikemen near walls to intercept, or focus ballista fire early.
Deranged goblins strapped with volatile explosives. They cackle as they charge.
Kill them before they reach the walls. A single ballista shot is enough — don't let groups build up.
The mightiest warlords of the orc clans. Their presence rallies lesser orcs to fight harder.
Bosses have massive HP pools. Focus all firepower on the boss immediately — its high damage will shred walls fast.
Goblin siege engineers who lob explosive shells from extreme range.
Cannoneers have low HP but attack from far away. Use crossbowmen or a well-aimed ballista to pick them off quickly.
Lore Unlocked: {0}/6  |  Kill enemies to reveal tactical tips
Complete runs to earn Legacy Points and unlock bonuses.

Press a key to bind...

Press Escape to cancel

Press a gamepad button to bind...

Press Escape to cancel
Describe the bug... What were you doing? What happened vs what you expected?

You have been chosen to defend the Orcish Marches — a frontier stronghold under siege.

Waves of enemies will assault your fortress. Your job: hold the walls, command defenders, and keep the tower standing.

This tutorial will walk you through everything you need to know.

At the center of the map stands your Tower — if enemies reach it, the game is over. Surrounding the tower is a square ring of Walls with octagonal towers at each joint. Your menials and defenders can slip through the narrow gaps between towers, but enemies are too large to fit. Protect the walls. They are your first line of defense.

Use the Scroll Wheel to zoom in and out across the battlefield. The camera is centered on your fortress, giving you a full overhead view of the surrounding terrain. Enemies initially attack from the west, but the spawn arc widens each day — keep watch in all directions.

The bar at the top of the screen shows vital information: Gold — your currency for upgrades and defenders Menials — idle / total workforce count Defenders — count of each defender type Phase Timer — elapsed game time Enemy Count — remaining / total enemies this day Kills — total enemies slain Day/Night Wheel — shows the current cycle position

The game alternates between Day and Night phases. Day (Attack Phase) Enemies spawn and assault your fortress. Each day the spawn arc widens, threatening more of your walls. Enemies grow stronger with each passing day. Night (Build Phase) Once all enemies are cleared and the sun sets, a Build Phase begins. Use this time to place new walls, recruit defenders, and prepare for the next assault.

When the Build Phase begins at sunset, wall placement starts automatically (if you have an engineer and enough gold). Time slows to 10% during build mode — all units and timers run at reduced speed while you plan your defenses. A green ghost wall follows your cursor. It snaps to existing walls when close enough. Left Click — place a wall (costs gold) {K:RotateWallLeft} / {K:RotateWallRight} — rotate the ghost wall {K:ToggleBuildMode} — exit build mode Right Click / {K:ExitBuildMode} — exit build mode The ghost turns red when you can't afford the next wall. Build mode ends automatically when you run out of gold.

Each new day brings a random Daily Event that modifies the rules. Beneficial: increased loot, stronger defenders, faster menials, or tougher walls. Detrimental: more enemy spawns, tougher enemies, faster foes, or siege damage. Mixed: a bonus paired with a drawback — for example, richer loot but more spawns. Watch for the event announcement at the start of each day and adapt your strategy.

Your primary weapon is the Ballista (scorpio) mounted on the tower. Left Click on the battlefield to aim and fire at the target location. You can purchase additional ballistas from the upgrade panel (up to 3 total). Press {K:SwitchBallista} to switch between them. On Easy difficulty, red aim lines show your firing direction. On Hard and above, a slight aim wobble adds challenge to every shot. Use the ballista to pick off dangerous enemies before they reach your walls.

Walls form a protective ring around your tower. Each segment is flanked by octagonal towers that connect to adjacent walls. Walls take damage from enemy attacks. When destroyed, enemies pour through the breach. New walls placed during Build Mode start under construction — an Engineer must walk over and build them before they become solid. Engineers also automatically repair damaged walls during combat.

Gold is your primary resource for purchasing upgrades and recruiting defenders. When enemies die, they drop Treasure on the ground. Right-click near treasure to send the nearest idle menial to collect it. They will pick up loot along their path, then return it to the fortress. In Nightmare mode, right-click dispatches a menial toward your crosshair. Spend wisely — there is never enough gold for everything.

Menials are your workforce — right-click near loot to send them out to collect it. Menials can also be recruited as Defenders through the upgrade panel. Each defender costs 2-3 menials who walk to the tower and convert. The HUD shows idle/total menials. Balance your workforce: too few menials means uncollected gold, too many recruited means no one to gather resources.

Press {K:ToggleUpgrades} to open the Upgrade Panel. Here you can spend gold (and menials) on: New Defenders — recruit menials into combat roles Ballista Upgrades — more damage, faster fire rate, or additional ballistas Keys {K:UpgradeSlot1}-{K:UpgradeSlot9} act as hotkeys for each upgrade button. Press {K:ToggleUpgrades} again or {K:OpenMenu} to close the panel.

Defenders are units that fight alongside you. Each type has a unique role: Engineer (30g, 2m) — repairs damaged walls, builds new ones Pikeman (40g, 2m) — melee fighter, engages enemies up close Crossbowman (50g, 2m) — ranged attacker, fires from a distance Wizard (100g, 3m) — powerful AoE magic, longest range Recruit them from the upgrade panel. Costs scale with how many are currently alive. You need at least one Engineer to enter Build Mode. Press {K:Recall} or Middle Click to Recall all defenders and menials to the courtyard.

You will face a variety of enemies: Orc Grunt — standard melee attacker Bow Orc — ranged attacker, fires arrows Troll — heavy hitter, smashes walls quickly Suicide Goblin — fast, explodes on contact Cannoneer — long-range siege unit Orc War Boss — massive, devastating; appears around day 10 Enemies that remain when night falls will retreat, but return the next day.

Refugees arrive from the wilds outside your fortress. If they reach the tower, they join as Menials — adding to your workforce. Some refugees carry Power-ups (shown by a colored glow). These grant your ballista special abilities like Double Shot or Burst Damage. Protect them from enemies on their way in.

Bushes and Trees grow in the wilderness. Vegetation spreads at night, filling the battlefield over time. Trees block enemy and defender pathing. Menials will automatically clear vegetation that blocks their path while collecting loot.

If a wall segment is destroyed, enemies will breach your defenses and rush toward the tower. If any enemy reaches the Tower, the game is over. Keep your walls standing, station defenders near weak points, and use the ballista to thin enemy ranks before they arrive. When the enemy path is blocked by walls, they will focus their attacks on the weakest wall to try to break through.

At the end of each night (starting after day 1), you are offered a choice of 3 Relics. Relics provide permanent bonuses for the rest of the run. Pick one, or skip if none suit your strategy. Offensive: Orcish Whetstone, Sharpened Bolts, Battle Fury Defensive: Reinforced Mortar, Slowing Wards Economy: War Chest, Plunderer's Charm, Refugee's Beacon Risk/Reward: Blood Offering, Rapid Reload, Engineer's Toolkit, Orcish Trophy Many relics have tradeoffs — read carefully before choosing. Relics stack multiplicatively, so the same type of bonus compounds over multiple nights.

Before starting a run, you can select a Commander Class from the main menu. Each commander changes the rules for the entire run: Warden — walls +30% HP, wall cost -20%, but defenders cost +30% Captain — defenders +20% damage, cost -20%, but walls -20% HP Artificer — ballista upgrades -50% cost, +25% damage, but -1 starting menial Merchant — loot +40%, refugees +30% faster, but enemies +15% HP Or select None to play with default settings. Your choice persists between sessions until you change it.

Mutators are game-modifying rules you can toggle from the main menu. Each mutator changes the gameplay significantly and applies a score multiplier: Blood Tide — +50% enemies, +30% loot (x1.5) Iron March — enemies move 30% faster (x1.3) Glass Fortress — walls half HP, defenders +50% damage (x1.4) Night Terrors — full waves spawn at night (x1.6) Lone Ballista — no defenders, ballista 2x damage (x1.8) Mutators unlock through achievements. Score multipliers stack (capped at 5.0x). Some mutators are incompatible and auto-disable each other.

Every run earns War Trophies — a permanent currency that persists across runs. Trophies earned = (days x 2) + (kills / 10) + (boss kills x 5). Minimum 1 per run. Spend trophies in the main menu UPGRADES panel on permanent bonuses: War Coffers — bonus starting gold Forged Tips / Oiled Gears — ballista damage and fire rate Swift Boots — faster menials Reinforced Foundations — wall HP Keen Eye — loot value Volunteer Corps — extra starting menial Upgrades have multiple levels with scaling costs. Bonuses apply automatically at run start.

The game tracks your progress through two reward systems: Achievements have Bronze, Silver, and Gold tiers across 5 categories (Survival, Combat, Economy, Defender, Special). Gold-tier achievements unlock mutators. Milestones are 26 one-time challenges that award War Trophies (3-15 each) on first completion. Examples: survive 10 days on Hard, kill 500 enemies, defeat a boss. Your Legacy Rank also accumulates over time, providing small permanent bonuses (starting gold, menial speed, ballista damage, and more). Check your progress from the main menu: ACHIEVEMENTS, STATISTICS, BESTIARY, and LEGACY.

On Nightmare difficulty, you experience the battle from the scorpio itself. The camera switches to a first-person view mounted on the ballista. Move the mouse to aim — the scorpio rotates and tilts to follow your aim. Bolts fire in a gravity arc and pass through friendly walls, so aim above distant targets. There are no aim guides — trust your instincts. An atmospheric mist obscures the battlefield beyond scorpio range. Enemies loom out of the fog — watch for movement and fire early. During Build Mode, the camera switches back to the overhead view for wall placement, then returns to FPS when building ends. Nightmare uses Hard mode enemy stats with aim wobble. Press {K:SwitchBallista} to switch between ballistas. Right-click dispatches menials toward your crosshair.

You can save your game mid-run and resume later. Press {K:OpenMenu} to open the pause menu, then select SAVE & QUIT. Choose one of 3 save slots — the game state is saved and you return to the main menu. To resume, press CONTINUE on the main menu and pick your save slot. Each slot shows the day number, difficulty, gold, and timestamp. What gets saved: day/night phase, gold, walls (with HP), ballistas, enemies, defenders, menials, loot on the ground, upgrades, relics, mutators, commander, daily events, and run statistics. Auto-save: On mobile, the game automatically saves when you switch apps or go to the home screen — your progress is safe if the OS reclaims memory. Note: Menials resume in an idle state after loading. Right-click loot to re-dispatch them.

Hotkey Summary: Scroll Wheel — Zoom in/out Left Click — Fire ballista (or place wall in build mode) Right Click — Send menial to collect loot {K:Recall} / Middle Click — Recall all defenders and menials {K:SwitchBallista} — Switch active ballista {K:ToggleUpgrades} — Open/close upgrade panel {K:UpgradeSlot1}-{K:UpgradeSlot9} — Upgrade hotkeys {K:RotateWallLeft} / {K:RotateWallRight} — Rotate wall in build mode {K:ToggleBuildMode} — Enter/exit build mode {K:Pause} — Toggle pause {K:OpenMenu} — Pause menu / exit build mode All controls can be rebound from Options > Input Bindings. Main Menu Features: CONTINUE — resume a saved game COMMANDER — choose a class before starting MUTATORS — toggle game modifiers UPGRADES — spend War Trophies on permanent bonuses BESTIARY / STATISTICS / ACHIEVEMENTS / LEGACY Good luck, Commander. The Marches depend on you!
